key_insight: In paramagnetic transition-metal oxides, the metal-insulator state is controlled by competition between spin-disorder-driven moment variation and off-center positional symmetry breaking.
engineering_relevance: This provides a symmetry-based design rule for predicting and tuning conductivity in oxide materials without relying on strong-correlation models.
method: Used symmetry-broken density-functional theory (DFT) to model paramagnetic phases.
Compared insulating NaFeO3 with metallic NaOsO3.
Analyzed how spin disorder and polymorphous atomic displacements jointly affect the band gap.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a direct DFT comparison of two oxides showing contrasting paramagnetic outcomes: insulating NaFeO3 versus metallic NaOsO3.
watch_item: The framework is derived from specific oxide examples and should be validated across broader materials classes before general design use.
paper_title: Spin disorder competing with positional symmetry breaking governs the metal-insulator behavior in oxide paramagnets
